source: BEP Research — compiled from public pricing pages, earnings calls, SEC filings, press releases
methodology: Token Cost Waterfall maps the margin stack from GPU silicon to end-customer. Three layers: (1) Model Provider — what it costs to produce and sell tokens, (2) Enterprise Platform — what SaaS companies pay model providers and charge customers, (3) End Customer — effective cost per AI interaction. Assumptions flagged with confidence levels.

model_providers.description: What model providers charge per token via API. Source: OpenRouter live pricing.

enterprise_platforms.description: What enterprise SaaS platforms charge customers for AI features, and what they pay model providers.
enterprise_platforms.data_source: Public pricing pages, earnings calls, analyst estimates

end_customer.description: Effective cost per AI interaction for the end customer after platform markup.
end_customer.data_source: Estimated from platform pricing and typical usage patterns

customerPricing.notes: M365 Copilot. Unlimited AI queries within subscription.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Assumes GPT-4o class at $10/M output with 50% volume discount = $5/M effective. 15 interactions/day × 4K tokens × 30 days.

margins.notes: Microsoft likely gets significant volume discount from OpenAI (49% investor). Actual margin could be higher.

platforms.name: GitHub Copilot

customerPricing.notes: Individual plan. Business at $19/user/month. Enterprise at $39/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Heavy usage: 40 completions/day × 8K tokens. Multi-model routing (GPT-4o + Sonnet). Blended cost ~$8/M output. At this usage, GitHub Copilot may be loss-making per user.

margins.notes: DATA GAP: Actual usage varies wildly. Light users (~10 completions/day) are profitable. Heavy users are deeply subsidized. GitHub disclosed Copilot is approaching profitability in aggregate as of late 2025.

platforms.dataGap: Actual per-user token consumption is not disclosed. Margin estimate is highly usage-dependent.

platforms.name: ServiceNow Now Assist

customerPricing.notes: Now Assist is an add-on to existing ServiceNow licenses. Pricing varies by module. $600M+ ACV as of Q4 2025. Estimated $40-60/user/month.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Uses Claude as default model for Build Agent (Anthropic partnership). Mixed with proprietary smaller models for routing/classification. 8 interactions/day is conservative for IT workflow automation.

margins.notes: ServiceNow has strong pricing power — enterprise customers locked into platform. McDermott bought $23M personal shares. Margin likely higher due to model routing (cheap models for simple queries).

platforms.dataGap: Now Assist pricing is not publicly listed. ACV disclosed but per-user breakdown is estimated.

customerPricing.notes: Agentforce pricing: $2 per conversation. This is a usage-based model, not per-seat.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: ~10K tokens per conversation (multi-turn agent interaction). Blended model cost ~$12/M using mix of GPT-4o and proprietary xGen for routing. Volume discount likely.

margins.notes: Extraordinary margin. Salesforce charges $2/conversation against ~$0.12 model cost. This is a 94% gross margin on AI — the platform distribution premium in action.

customerPricing.notes: Zendesk AI agent pricing: $1 per automated resolution. Only charged when AI fully resolves a ticket without human handoff.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: ~8K tokens per resolution (multi-turn support conversation). Uses GPT-4o class with proprietary fine-tuning. Volume discount from OpenAI likely.

margins.notes: Similar to Salesforce — enormous markup on the model cost. Enterprise distribution is the margin, not the AI.

customerPricing.notes: Fin AI agent: $0.99 per resolution. Fin AI Copilot for agents included in plans.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: ~6K tokens per resolution. Multi-model (GPT-4o + Claude for quality). Volume discount.

margins.notes: Near-identical margin to Zendesk. The AI agent per-resolution model is the highest-margin pricing model in enterprise SaaS.

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI (Platform markup)

customerPricing.notes: Vertex AI charges same as direct Gemini API pricing. No platform markup on own models. Third-party models (Claude, Llama) carry small markup.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Google is vertically integrated — no external model provider cost. Margin is GPU cost to API price.

margins.notes: Vertically integrated. Google's margin is the inference margin from our GPU tracker — hardware cost to API price. No platform layer.

platforms.dataGap: Vertex markup on third-party models not publicly disclosed.

platforms.name: Azure OpenAI Service

customerPricing.notes: Azure OpenAI pricing matches or slightly exceeds direct OpenAI API pricing. Enterprise customers often get volume discounts through EAs.

estimatedModelCost.assumptions: Microsoft takes a revenue share from OpenAI API usage on Azure. Estimated 20-30% of token revenue goes to Microsoft as platform fee.

margins.notes: Microsoft keeps estimated 20-30% of OpenAI API revenue on Azure as platform/infra fee. OpenAI keeps the rest. Source: partnership structure disclosed in SEC filings.

platforms.dataGap: Exact revenue share between Microsoft and OpenAI is not publicly confirmed.

token_to_api.description: Token production cost → API sell price

waterfall_summary.key_finding: Margin concentrates at the distribution layer, not the production layer. Enterprise platforms capture 90%+ margins on AI features while model providers operate at 20-60% margins and GPU providers at single-digit margins on commodity hardware. The platform is the moat, not the model.
